title: 7 takeaways from A.J. Brown’s first press conference with the Patriots
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: A.J. Brown expressed excitement about joining the Patriots and playing for his childhood team.
Brown has switched to wearing No. 1 with the Patriots, despite Julian Edelman's invitation to wear No. 11.
Concerns over Brown's knee did not deter the Patriots deal; Brown assured there are no injury worries.

title: Police seek public’s help in finding missing 14-year-old Brighton boy
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Fari Carless, 14, from Brighton, was last seen on May 1 near Jette Court and reported missing on May 19.
Carless is described as a 5’4” Black male with a thin build, long dreadlocks, and brown eyes.
Boston police urge anyone with information to contact them at 617-343-4256 or provide tips anonymously via CrimeStoppers.

title: As the weather heats up again, here are the Mass. beaches closed Tuesday
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Beaches closed in Ashby, Dartmouth, Natick, Plymouth, and Shutesbury as of June 2, 2026, according to DPH.
Dartmouth beaches closed due to CSO/SSO events from heavy rain impacting sewage systems, says public health department.
Natick beach shut due to harmful cyanobacteria bloom that poses health risks, DPH water quality dashboard reports.

title: What Mike Vrabel said about A.J. Brown’s skillset, cost, and rocky exit from Philly
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots traded a 2028 first-round pick and 2027 fifth-round pick to acquire A.J. Brown from the Eagles.
A.J. Brown ranks fourth in NFL for receiving yards (8,029) and touchdown catches (56) since 2019.
Mike Vrabel not concerned with Brown's exit from Eagles; focuses on his impact as a Patriots receiver.

title: Gov. Healey backs bill to keep Mass. bars open until 3 a.m. this summer
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey supports a bill allowing Massachusetts bars to remain open until 3 a.m. during summer 2026.
The bill would permit sales extensions from June 1 to August 31, coinciding with events like the FIFA World Cup.
The proposal, filed by Rep. Carole Fiola, needs approval from the House and Senate before becoming law.

title: A.J. Brown makes his Patriots practice debut and other takeaways from Day 2 of OTAs
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: A.J. Brown made his practice debut with the Patriots during Day 2 of OTAs at Gillette Stadium.
Quarterback Maye delivered an accurate, turnover-free performance, highlighted by deep completions to Eli Raridon and DeMario Douglas.
Tight-end Julian Hill was placed on injured reserve and is not expected to play this season, per Coach Vrabel.

title: Market Basket’s ex-CEO, Arthur T. Demoulas, says he won’t fight court ruling upholding his firing
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Arthur T. Demoulas won't appeal the court decision upholding his removal as Market Basket CEO.
Demoulas's sisters, controlling 61% of the company, allegedly orchestrated his ousting amid familial tensions.
The Delaware court ruled his removal valid due to board disagreements and past leadership conflicts.

title: MBTA and Boston announce plans for Summer Street closures during World Cup
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: MBTA and Boston will close Summer Street to vehicles during select World Cup match days for fan coordination.
Closures affect Summer Street between Dorchester and Atlantic avenues for eight hours on specific match days.
MBTA will remove its eminent domain letter and receive necessary permits from Boston for temporary security setups.

title: Man allegedly involved in wrong-way Route 1 crash detained by ICE before arraignment
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Lucas Gustavo Brajak DeAlmeida Di Benedetto was detained by ICE outside Peabody District Court before his arraignment for a wrong-way crash.
Di Benedetto, 41, was charged with OUI and negligent operation after allegedly driving south in the northbound lane on Route 1.
The crash resulted in minor injuries to a State Police trooper and occurred weeks after another fatal wrong-way crash in Lynnfield.

title: All the new movies and TV shows streaming in June
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: HBO Max releases 'House of the Dragon' Season 3 on June 21, with Matt Smith and Emma D'Arcy starring.
Disney+ features non-summer blockbusters 'Hoppers' and 'Avatar: Fire and Ash' debuting on June 3 and June 24.
New original soccer series 'USA 94: Brazil’s Return to Glory' and 'Ultras: Passion and Death' premiere on Netflix and HBO Max.
